transcript: These noodles taste so good. Thanks to our sponsor OxiClean MaxForce Laundry Stain Remover, I'm showing you a whole new take on spaghetti that kids will love to eat and help you make. We're talking about rainbow pasta. OxiClean MaxForce Laundry Stain Remover removes the toughest stains, even buttered noodles, so it's okay to get colorful in the kitchen. We're going to start by making parmesan cheese twills. They're a super simple recipe and they're one that you can use in a ton of different dishes. We're going to coat our skillet with non-stick cooking spray and then place four piles of parmesan on the skillet. Heat it over medium heat for four to six minutes or until the cheese has melted and the edges have turned golden brown. Remove from the heat and let it cool before transferring them to a paper towel lined plate. Thanks for watching! Heat it over medium heat for 4 to 6 minutes or until the cheese has melted and the edges have turned golden brown. Remove from the heat and let it cool before transferring them to a paper towel lined plate. Now we're going to make homemade food coloring using all natural ingredients like turmeric, cabbage and beets. Let's start with pink. So add the raw beets to a food processor and pulse about 10 times until they're coarsely chopped. Transfer the beets to a saucepan and add 2 cups of water and simmer on medium-high heat for about 5 minutes. Once the water becomes a deep, vibrant pink color, pour the liquid through a cheesecloth-lined strainer and set aside to cool. Next up, we're going to make yellow with turmeric. In a clean saucepan, we're going to combine our turmeric root with 3 cups of water. Bring to a simmer for 5 5 minutes and when the water is a yellow-orange, strain it and let it cool. Finally, we're going to make purple with red cabbage. Add your cabbage to a heavy bottom pot and cover with 5 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil and then lower the temperature to a simmer and let it simmer for about 8 minutes or until the water turns a deep purple color. Once it's done, strain and separate into two bowls to cool. So the best part of this recipe is when we get to put on our lab coat and play chemist in the kitchen. So cabbage juice has a cool property. It's an acid-base indicator, meaning depending on if you add an acid or a base to it, it changes color. So in this case we have our cabbage juice and I'm just gonna add some baking soda oh my gosh it's magic oh it turned to blue magic or science and science rules now we have our four colors we have our blue red purple and yellow but I think I want green and according to the color wheel blue and yellow make green so I'm just gonna put a third of the yellow into this other bowl and a third of the blue Great, we have all of our colors and now it's time to dye our pasta. So I'm just going to divide up our al dente spaghetti into five zippered bags and then pour one color in each bag and let the noodles soak up the color for at least 20 minutes or up until two hours. While we're letting those sit, we're going to our sauce. In a sauce pot over medium-high heat, we're gonna combine our butter, garlic, shallots, chives, and thyme until everything is melted. Bring it to a boil and then reduce to a simmer for about six minutes or until the butter is slightly brown. Remove from the heat and add your lemon zest, juice, and a pinch of salt. Then we're just going to strain this mixture through some cheesecloth to get rid of the extra herbs. I cannot wait to see how this pasta is going to turn out. Once the pastas are ready, drain each batch on paper towel lined plates or sheet trays. Then just mix them together and serve with some freshly grated parmesan and those cheese twills. This pasta looks so amazing. You guys know I love rainbow anything, but the fact that every single noodle is a different Thanks for watching! with some freshly grated parmesan and those cheese twills. This pasta looks so amazing. You guys know I love rainbow anything, but the fact that every single noodle is a different flavor, I cannot wait to try. These noodles are so good, but I love this sauce. It's so herbaceous and zesty and bright. I cannot wait to make this for my nephews or even my next dinner party. You guys let me know if you enjoyed this video. Give me a thumbs up and subscribe to PopSugarFood and tag someone that you want to make this for you. Why not? In the comment box below. I'm Brandi and I'll see you next time. Bye.
